title: 16th FC Review: Centre, State, Local
lead: Constituted under ==Art 280==, chaired by ==Dr Arvind Panagariya== — operating amid an upcoming ==delimitation==, post-COVID fiscal stress, ==GST rationalisation (Sept 2025)==, rising ‘freebie’ spending, and Centre–State friction over ==cesses eating the shareable pool==.

head: Area
Recommendation
Significance
rows: Continuity — but cesses/surcharges still shrink the effective pool.
==No revenue deficit, sector- or state-specific grants==
Major departure — pushes states towards own reforms.
Strongest-ever focus on the third tier.
States' FD capped at ==3% of GSDP==; off-budget borrowing discouraged

items: ==41% of a shrinking pool== ≠ 41% of total Union tax effort — hence the CAG-disclosure recommendation.
==GDP-contribution criterion== (moderated by ==square root of GSDP==) rewards larger economies without overwhelming equity; ==southern states' combined share improved== vs the 15th FC.
But GSDP reflects ==history, geography, ports, agglomeration== — not just current policy; dropping tax-effort may weaken revenue-mobilisation incentives.

==Gap-filling grants abolished==: discourages permanent dependence — but may hurt states with ==genuine structural disadvantages== (hilly terrain, disaster-prone, low tax base). ==Art 275 grants== could serve as an equalisation tool; a distinction between avoidable deficits and structural gaps was needed.

blocks.text: ==Entry-level conditions==: local bodies duly constituted (Parts IX/IX-A); ==accounts online==; SFCs constituted regularly with ATRs laid before legislatures within 6 months — a strong institutional reform linking ==money to elections, accounts and transparency==. Basic:performance split ==80:20==; RLB:ULB split ==60:40==.

items.title: Urban — Big Recognition, With Conditions
bullets: Urban share raised to ==45%== (vs ~36% under 15th FC) — ==highest in FC history==; more untied grants
==Special Infrastructure Component==: ₹56,100 crore for wastewater management in growth centres (10–40 lakh population)
==Urbanisation Premium==: ₹10,000 crore one-time incentive for planned ==rural-to-urban transition==
Distribution: 90% projected urban population + ==10% OSR-linked==; performance grants tied to OSR growth; ==Service Level Benchmarks== with third-party audit

items.title: Rural — Continuity With Discipline
bullets: ==OSR targets for Gram Panchayats==: 1.025× previous year or 2.5% CAGR (whichever lower), benchmark ==₹1,200/household/year==
Gram Sabha resolutions required — ==strengthens local democracy==; but OSR benchmarks may penalise poor, tribal, hilly Panchayats

items: ==Tied/untied design==: 50% of basic grants tied to ==sanitation, solid waste and water==; rest untied — max 20% of untied on roads, ==none on salaries==. Balanced, but over-conditionality and delayed releases could erode autonomy in practice.

==Concerns==: conditionality burden on weak ULBs; OSR benchmarks hard for poorer municipalities; premature village mergers for the premium; ==limited climate focus== (heat, flooding missing); state control over municipal powers remains high.
==Reform cesses via a federal compact== — sunset clauses, utilisation disclosure, conversion into shareable taxes
==Targeted equalisation grants== (Art 275) for structural disabilities in health, education, climate
Improve the formula — a composite ==‘fiscal capacity and service need’ index==
==Strengthen SFCs== — permanent secretariats, timelines, public databases
==Municipal finance capacity== — GIS property tax, municipal cadres, audited accounts
==Support weaker Panchayats== — training, digital accounting, social audit
Subsidies: ==transparent, targeted, time-bound== with fiscal-impact statements
Link disaster finance to ==climate resilience== — flood control, heat action plans
A standing ==Fiscal Federalism Council== reviewing cesses, CSS, devolution annually

blocks.text: The way forward is not merely more money, but a more transparent, predictable and genuinely federal fiscal architecture.
